title: Jackiw-Rebbi Zero Mode For A Sign-Changing Dirac Mass
summary: If a Dirac mass profile changes sign across an interface, then the Dirac operator admits a normalizable wall-localized zero mode whose envelope is the exponential of minus the mass integral.
assumptions: The asymptotic masses at x_1 to plus and minus infinity have opposite sign.
The tangential momentum is tuned to the interface zero-mode branch when solving the transverse localization problem.
regime: Continuum Dirac operator with a one-dimensional sign-changing mass profile.
scope: Reusable source-grade zero-mode theorem behind the Lecture Three domain-wall derivation.

source_anchors.notes: Original source of the sign-changing-mass zero-mode mechanism.
Witten uses the Jackiw-Rebbi mechanism in the folded Hall-boundary problem.

representation: Source-grade zero-mode theorem extracted from the sign-changing-mass Dirac equation.
motivation: This note exists because the Jackiw-Rebbi mechanism is a reusable theorem in its own right, not only a citation inside the Hall-boundary story. It is the exact local statement that turns a sign-changing mass profile into a localized zero mode.
prerequisite_story: Know the folded sign-changing-mass Dirac equation, the separation ansatz for the transverse profile, and the normalizability criterion for a wall-localized state.
local_context_story: In the current Lecture Three branch, this theorem is the source-grade local zero-mode result inserted between the wall-equation derivation and the anomaly-inflow interpretation.
forward_links_story: After the zero mode is established, the Callan-Harvey inflow equation explains why that boundary mode is precisely what the bulk Hall response requires for gauge consistency.
canonical_questions: What exact sign condition on the mass profile guarantees the zero mode?
How does the exponential envelope arise from the wall Dirac equation?
Why is this theorem a local ingredient rather than the whole edge-mode consistency story?
scope_boundaries: This theorem establishes the local wall zero mode but does not by itself compute the anomaly it carries.
The theorem is continuum and local to the interface; it is not the same statement as a lattice bulk-boundary correspondence theorem.

math_expressions.notes: Dirac equation with a wall-dependent mass profile.
Normalizable envelope when the mass flips sign.

symbols.meaning: Dirac mass profile across the interface
transverse localization envelope of the zero mode

failure_modes: If the mass does not change sign, the exponential profile fails to decay on both sides and this zero-mode construction is not normalizable.
admissible_assumptions: Work with a continuum Dirac operator whose mass profile has opposite asymptotic signs on the two sides of the interface.
Treat the tangential zero-mode branch and the wall ansatz as accepted setup data for the theorem.
formalization_blockers: The analytic existence statement for localized zero modes of sign-changing-mass Dirac operators is not yet packaged as a reusable Lean theorem.
The relation between the local zero mode and the later anomaly-carrying chiral boundary theory remains a separate bridge theorem.
retrieval_hints: Use when the answer must reconstruct the actual Jackiw-Rebbi mode instead of citing the paper name only.
